title: eval-integrity audit result
description: Machine-readable result of a benchmark-credibility audit. The skill writes one of these alongside the markdown report so audits can be diffed over time (CI trend-tracking). Sub-check statuses roll up to a dimension status; dimension statuses roll up to the verdict.

audited_commit.description: Full or short git SHA the audit was run against. Use 'uncommitted' if the working tree was dirty.

date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the audit was produced.

repo.description: Path or name of the audited benchmark repo. Optional context, not load-bearing.

verdict.description: Overall roll-up. publish_ready = no INVALIDATING gaps. not_publish_ready = one or more INVALIDATING gaps. gradable_with_caveats = no INVALIDATING gaps but HARDENING gaps remain (numbers are defensible, credibility could be higher).

dimensions.description: One entry per audit dimension. Keys are the fixed dimension names below.

status.description: PRESENT = implemented AND enforced (code or test). PARTIAL = documented or half-built, not enforced. ABSENT = no evidence. N/A = the dimension or sub-check does not apply (e.g. judge validity on a benchmark with no LLM judge).

severity.description: Consequence of the gap. INVALIDATING = a reviewer can throw out a published number. HARDENING = weakens credibility, does not invalidate. NONE = no gap (status PRESENT or N/A).

evidence.description: One piece of evidence: a file, an optional line, and a note. Absence evidence uses the searched-locations note with file set to the search scope.

file.description: Repo-relative path the evidence lives in, or a search scope for absence findings (e.g. 'searched: run_eval.py, .github/, docs/').

line.description: 1-based line number, or null when not line-specific (whole-file, directory, or absence evidence).

note.description: What this evidence shows. For ABSENT, state what was searched and found nothing — a grep miss alone is not enough; note the concept search.

sub_check.description: One individually-scoreable check within a dimension. This granularity is deliberate: a dimension is a roll-up of its sub-checks, not a single verdict.

id.description: Stable sub-check ID (e.g. 'PR1', 'CT3'). Matches the IDs in the dimension's methodology file.

evidence.description: Evidence backing the status. Mandatory for any non-PRESENT status: cite file:line or state the concept search that found nothing.

fix.description: Concrete change that would close the gap (file to add, guard to write, test to add). null when status is PRESENT or N/A.

dimension.description: A dimension result: the rolled-up status plus the sub-checks it was computed from.

severity.description: Worst severity across the dimension's sub-check gaps. INVALIDATING if any sub-check is an INVALIDATING gap.

sub_checks.description: The individually-scored sub-checks. A dimension is PRESENT only when every applicable sub-check is PRESENT; PARTIAL when some are; ABSENT when none are.
